Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.

Social Anxiety/Selective Mutism Disorder: Fear of Social Situations - Current
Notes: 0= No information; 1= Not present; 2= Subthreshold: Clearly self-conscious and uncomformatble in social performance situations; avoids only 1 or 2 activites that are not critical to the child's wellbeing (e.g., avoiding large parties where child knows no one); 3= Threshold: Considerable self-consiousness that makes the child uncomfortable in several social setting; at least 1 activity is avoided. A marked and persistent fear of social performance situations - fears acting in a way (or shoing anxiety symptoms) that will be humiliating or embarrassing. DO NOT CODE AS THRESHOLD IF THE CHILD'S ONLY FEAR IS GIVING ORAL PRESENTATIONS AT SCHOOL
